SRS Generator Skill

This Copilot skill generates a complete Software Requirements Specification (SRS) in Markdown and converts it to PDF.

Usage

1. Open Copilot Chat and invoke the skill by name:
   /srs-generator

2. Answer the interactive prompts for project metadata, features, requirements, and other sections. The skill will ask follow-ups until required fields are filled.

3. Outputs are saved under `outputs/srs/YYYY-MM-DD/` with two files:
   - `<project-slug>_SRS.md` (the SRS in Markdown)
   - `<project-slug>_SRS.pdf` (generated by pandoc)

Prerequisites

- pandoc installed and available on PATH. For best PDF results install a TeX engine (e.g., TeX Live or TinyTeX) or provide a PDF engine supported by pandoc.
- If pandoc is not available, the skill will still write Markdown but cannot create the PDF.

Script

Run the included `generate_srs.sh` to convert the Markdown to PDF. Make it executable first:

```bash
chmod +x .github/skills/srs-generator/generate_srs.sh
.github/skills/srs-generator/generate_srs.sh path/to/md path/to/pdf
```

Notes

- The skill writes Markdown only from Copilot; the script converts that markdown to PDF. Keep the SRS content in the Markdown file for easy diffing and review.

Example one-shot invocation from Copilot Chat or CLI:

```
/srs-generator Project: Acme Payments; Version: 0.1; Authors: Alice Smith; Date: today; Intro: Purpose: build a payments API; Features: FR-1 Payment processing 3h high Inputs: payment request Outputs: confirmation Acceptance: payments accepted and recorded; done
```

After completion the skill will write `<project-slug>_SRS.md` and attempt to produce `<project-slug>_SRS.pdf` in `outputs/srs/YYYY-MM-DD/`. If no PDF engine is available, an HTML fallback will be produced instead.
